text: [claude] Refined requirement 4 (mismatch detection) per user feedback: now explicit about what MUST be gone after reset (issues, deps, labels, comments created after checkpoint) and what MUST survive (all pre-checkpoint data with correct fields). Added round-trip integrity check (reset backward then forward, verify no corruption/duplication). This is the highest-value test in the suite.

text: [claude] Revised Phase 1 approach: mismatch detection moves from post-checkout hook to PersistentPreRun. Pairs git reset (destructive) with Dolt reset (destructive) — correct semantics. git checkout (detached HEAD) deferred to Phase 2. Created bd-vlu.1.7 as the critical code change; bd-vlu.1.3/1.4/1.5 now blocked on it.

(3) postCheckoutBeadsSync() removed from hooks.go. (4) Tests: 4 cases covering reset round-trip, in-sync no-op, no-refs graceful skip, silent mode. (5) Non-TTY fallback. All acceptance criteria met. Recommendation: Close.

author: Bryan Hirsch

author: Bryan Hirsch
text: [claude] Review of bd-2qt: Completed: (1) bd reset command wraps git reset + immediate checkBeadsRefSync(). (2) bd check-refs standalone mismatch detection. (3) Both registered on rootCmd. Build passes. Remaining: (1) Exit code gap — help text says exit 1 for silent-mode mismatch, but checkBeadsRefSync() is void and always exits 0. (2) No tests. Recommendation: Not ready to close. Fix exit code doc/behavior mismatch first (simplest: remove exit 1 claim from help text).

author: Bryan Hirsch
text: [claude] Remediation complete: (1) Fixed check-refs help text — removed incorrect exit code 1 claim, replaced with clear behavior description matching the 4-step logic (compare refs, check settings, follow/prompt strategy, execute). (2) Added git_reset_test.go with 11 tests: command registration (2), checkBeadsRefSync paths including auto-reset, silent divergence, branch mismatch, nil store (4), readBeadsRefs edge cases (4), truncHash (1). Dolt-dependent tests skip without server as expected. Build+vet clean.

author: Bryan Hirsch
text: [claude] Review: isRebaseInProgress() worktree fix | PASS. The fix correctly replaces hardcoded .git/rebase-merge and .git/rebase-apply paths with filepath.Join(gitDir, ...) using git.GetGitDir(). This resolves the worktree case where .git is a file, not a directory. Comment explains the rationale clearly. Verdict: in_progress:review_passed

author: Bryan Hirsch
text: [claude] Review: isTerminal() stdin check | PASS. Changed from checking only os.Stderr to checking both os.Stdin and os.Stderr. Since prompts read from stdin and write to stderr, both must be TTYs. The loop approach is clean. Minor note: hooks.go:862 comment still says 'merge strategy (A/B/C)' — stale from old naming, but pre-existing and outside scope. Verdict: in_progress:review_passed

author: Bryan Hirsch
text: [claude] Review: config.yaml auto-creation | PASS with concern. findProjectConfigYaml() now auto-creates a bare config.yaml if .beads/ exists but config.yaml doesn't. Solves onboarding gap. Concern: file created with 0600 permissions — fine for security but other beads files may use 0644. Not a blocker. Verdict: in_progress:review_passed

author: Bryan Hirsch
text: [claude] Review: Quick fixes (comment, dead code, gitattributes) | PASS. Three fixes, all correct: (1) store.go comment updated from A/B/C to actual slug strings, (2) unreachable return after os.Exit(1) removed in vc.go, (3) .gitattributes marks .beads/HEAD and .beads/refs/** as linguist-generated. Verdict: in_progress:review_passed

author: Bryan Hirsch
text: [claude] Review: E2E bd reset integration test | NEEDS REFINEMENT. Bead description asks for E2E test of bd reset --hard HEAD~1 against a real git repo with Dolt state verification and round-trip integrity. What was delivered is TestCleanupStaleBeadsRefsInGitRepo — which tests ref file cleanup, not bd reset. TestAutoResetRoundTrip exists but skips when Dolt is unavailable. Next steps: (1) update bead description to match actual delivery, OR (2) acknowledge E2E reset test requires Docker/Dolt and existing TestAutoResetRoundTrip covers this with a note, OR (3) note that the comment references bd-vlu.1.15's scope not 1.14's. Verdict: in_progress:rework_needed — description/delivery mismatch. Clarify scope.

author: Bryan Hirsch
text: [claude] Review: Test cleanupStaleBeadsRefs in git repo | PASS. TestCleanupStaleBeadsRefsInGitRepo does exactly what the bead describes: inits a real git repo, creates and commits ref files, runs cleanupStaleBeadsRefs, and verifies both disk removal and staged deletions in the git index. Test passes. Verdict: in_progress:review_passed

author: Bryan Hirsch
text: [claude] Review: GetBranchInfo error/not-found separation | PASS. All 4 callers fixed: maybePromptBranchStrategy (hooks.go), maybeSendMessage (branch_messaging.go), executePostCommitStrategy (branch_strategy.go), maybeMergeOnClose (branch_strategy.go). Each now logs the error distinctly before returning, rather than conflating (nil, err) with (nil, nil). GetBranchInfo return also improved to use errors.Is(err, sql.ErrNoRows) instead of string comparison (commit deef5eb1). Verdict: in_progress:review_passed

author: Bryan Hirsch
text: [claude] label: in_progress:wip -> in_progress:review_needed | TestResetRoundTripWithGitRepo added to cmd/bd/git_reset_test.go. Test creates a real git repo, initializes DoltStore against Docker container, builds two Dolt commits with git commits, exercises git reset --hard HEAD~1 + checkBeadsRefSync, verifies both git refs and Dolt state moved backward, then resets forward verifying round-trip. Skips gracefully when Docker unavailable. All existing reset tests still pass.

author: Bryan Hirsch
text:
[claude] Implementation complete — 3 tests added across 2 commits:

Commit 65e9c09d — TestResetRoundTripWithGitRepo (bd-vlu.1.14 core deliverable):
- Creates real git repo + real Dolt container (Docker)
- Builds two Dolt+git commits with beads refs tracked
- git reset --hard HEAD~1 moves git back, checkBeadsRefSync detects mismatch and resets Dolt
- Verifies both git refs and Dolt state moved backward (issue B gone, issue A present)
- Resets forward, verifies round-trip integrity (both issues restored)
- Skips gracefully when Docker unavailable

Commit 53b16a32 — Additional coverage from PR #2473 review gaps:
- TestSlashedBranchRefRoundTrip: write→read→sync with feature/foo branch (nested refs/heads/feature/foo dir)
- TestCheckRefsBehavior: 3 subtests exercising bd check-refs beyond registration (in-sync no-op, mismatch auto-reset, disabled silent divergence)

All tests compile, pass (or skip gracefully without Docker), and existing tests unaffected.

author: Bryan Hirsch
text: [claude] label: review:criteria:in_progress -> review:criteria:1 | Criteria review complete — 5 criteria all specific/measurable/testable. Minor gap: no explicit regression criterion, but verified in comments.

author: Bryan Hirsch
text: [claude] label: review:technical:in_progress -> review:technical:1 | Technical review complete — code follows existing patterns, no new abstractions, no security/performance concerns. Minor note: TestCheckRefsBehavior subtests share mutable state (config + store), fragile if parallelized.

author: Bryan Hirsch
text: [claude] label: review:test:in_progress -> review:test:1 | Test review complete — all 3 new tests compile and skip gracefully without Docker. Full suite: 13 passed, 11 skipped (Docker-dependent), 0 failed. No regressions.

author: Bryan Hirsch
text: [claude] review:test — Docker test results: TestResetRoundTripWithGitRepo PASS, TestAutoResetRoundTrip PASS, TestCheckRefsBehavior PASS (3/3 subtests). TestSlashedBranchRefRoundTrip FAIL — writeTestBeadsRefs helper (hooks_checkout_test.go:412-416) doesn't MkdirAll for slashed branch parents. Fix: change line 412-415 to use filepath.Dir(refPath) instead of static refsDir.

author: Bryan Hirsch
text: [claude] review:test — Full Docker results: All 4 bd-vlu.1.14 tests PASS after writeTestBeadsRefs fix. 4 pre-existing test failures detected (TestCheckBeadsRefSyncSilentDivergence, TestCheckBeadsRefSyncDisabledNoAction, TestCheckBeadsRefSyncResetCorrectness, TestCheckBeadsRefSyncSilentMode) — these are shared-DB isolation issues, not caused by bd-vlu.1.14 changes.

author: Bryan Hirsch
text: [claude] label: review:interactive:in_progress -> review:interactive:1 | Interactive review complete. Two issues found and fixed: (1) writeTestBeadsRefs didn't handle slashed branches (ef3b65b6), (2) 4 tests used hardcoded 'main' branch instead of store's actual branch + wrong AddComment method (26c92416). All tests now pass with Docker/Dolt.
